item.title: Feature Highlight - Skitarii Character Customization
item.description:
Rising above the shackles of the flesh, the Skitarii have been rebuilt with the divine augments of the Adeptus Mechanicus to best serve the Omnissiah, as an impossibly efficient tool of war.

In case you missed it, check out the reveal trailer here.

This is part of an ongoing series of feature highlights focused on the upcoming DLC class: The Skitarii.

In our previous highlight we went over the overall vision of the Skitarii’s visual design! This feature highlight focuses on unique options available to the class as far as character creation and customization goes, from unique physical options to voice modification! We’ve interviewed our Associate Art Director, 3D Technical Artist, and Sound Designer to get you all the details!

“My name is Lucas. I am the Associate Art Director on Darktide and I’ve been involved in overseeing the art delivered in the production of the Skitarii class, working closely together with the different disciplines in our production development team. \[...] As a huge Warhammer fan myself, getting the opportunity to explore something from the Adeptus Mechanicus faction was very exciting. ~Lucas

“My name is Erik Lindqvist, I’m a Senior Technical Artist at Fatshark. I’ve been at the company for about 10 years, mainly working on Vermintide and Vermintide 2, and now I’ve also been leading work on Skitarii on the Technical Art side.” ~ Erik

“I'm Ola, I'm the VO Designer at Darktide. I'm in charge of wrangling all the VO we record, processing it and getting it to play in the game.  Sometimes I show up to bother Matt and the actors with strange requests in a recording session. Other than that I like to get my fingers in some UI code on occasion (Personality Scourge and this upcoming Voxcaster Tuning for example).” ~Ola

Not Quite Human Anymore

“When we started talking and discussing about how we’d go about presenting this class, a lot of our ideas went naturally to the character creation of it.” ~ Lucas

While Arbites and Hive Scum fit quite comfortably using the base ‘human’ body as the core classes, the same isn’t true for the Skitarii. Due to their cybernetic nature, there were a lot of visual aspects we wanted to represent that warranted a brand new body type to go with it. 

But this also gave us a unique opportunity to show something rarely seen in other Warhammer: 40,000 media, which is how they look under the robes and the hood, something that the entire Art team was eager to work on.

“There wasn’t really an established visual reference for what a Skitarii looks like underneath the robes and armor, so that was a mystery which we needed to solve. The idea of them being flesh fused with machine raised a lot of questions. And because this was such an interesting hook for us to think about, it naturally evolved into a focus for the class!” ~ Lucas

Because this is rarely depicted, we found most of the relevant information (and inspiration!) in written narrative mediums – books, lore excerpts in codices, and so on. While we always collaborate closely with Games Workshop when bringing all aspects of Warhammer: 40,000 to life in Darktide, this was especially true when working on the Skitarii character creation. 

“I don’t think we left any ideas out. It was more the other way around, that we added ideas as we went!” ~ Lucas 

We worked extensively on concepts and design, shaping together the look of these seldom explored facets of the Adeptus Mechanicus. Things like how their bodies look after such extensive modification, the type of scarring left on their flesh after all the assignments and environmental hazards they’re often subjected to. How exactly it looks where machine meets flesh.

Customizing your Skitarius

As you might expect, many of the customization options available to the Skitarii are very different from our other classes, in part because of the fusion of man and machine, but also because they’ll be almost completely covered up once you’re done. We had to figure out how to let you add your personal touch in ways that would still be visible under those bulky robes, after all!

“The character creation flow became very different. We cannot use skin colors, we cannot use faces in the same way, to express the character. So we need to find something else.” ~ Erik

Like our other classes, you’ll get to pick from several background options to shape your character’s history, and you’ll get to tweak their height (although they are on average taller than the regular human classes!). Additionally, you’ll also be able to pick your Skitarius’ Forge World of origin, which plays a similar role as the Hive Scum’s choice of Gang, such as altering your starting cosmetics!

One of these Forge World options, Branx Minor, is linked to Atoma itself!

Eye color

Their natural eyes have long been replaced with bionic upgrades, giving you several color options to choose from! There will be a primary and a secondary lens color option, and of course you can pick the same option for both to have the lenses look the same. Not only that, but these colors will show up no matter what headgear cosmetic you end up using for your Skitarius!

“And that is a bit different depending on the headgear. Some only have two eyes, so you’re basically picking the right and the left eye. Some have smaller diodes and things, as well. Some only have one, so then there’s only the Primary that’s showing.” ~ Erik

Mechanical Limbs

The Skitarii’s arms, legs, hands and feet have been upgraded with cybernetic replacements, and during character creation these will also be customizable! You will be able to choose different models for the arms and legs, and you will also get to pick from different metallic textures as the Primary and Secondary metals, including a separate setting to handle the oxidation level.

These customizations will also remain and be visible no matter what cosmetics you’re using, giving you more freedom to craft a unique look. And of course, the arms and hands will also show up in first person!

Voice Modulation

The Skitarii class will have access to four distinct personalities, something only the Ogryn class can match! But, taking advantage of the Skitarii’s cybernetic nature, we added the ability to customize how the voice itself can sound!

The voice modulation system found in certain headgear cosmetics gave us a solid starting point for the feature, but we pushed the concept further, giving the player different ways to tune it to their heart’s content and find the perfect sound.

There are three different settings that you can gradually tweak to modify the base voice of the chosen personality. As you play around with these dynamic parameters, you’ll get to listen to the changes in real time through the example voice lines playing from your chosen personality, or even switch personalities to hear how specific setups sound on all the voices!

As you might expect, one of these parameters controls the pitch… among other things! While there is some correlation with increasing this slider to make the pitch higher, or decreasing it to make the pitch lower, it is not so straightforward. There are various effects being influenced by this parameter, purposely making the pitch shift unpredictably in certain ranges, and sometimes making the normal, unmodified voice shine through.

The second parameter is a delay effect, which can sound like multiple voices at once, or like the voice has an almost immediate echo. Like with the pitch parameter, modifying this parameter is not as straightforward as it might seem, affecting not just the intensity of the effect, but also other sound effects linked to it to create some unpredictable variations.

“You will hear stuff change as you move this around, but our goal is also to… You know, we want you to stumble into something that sounds cool, rather than necessarily be like ‘Oh this goes up and this goes there’, you know. \[...] We modulate it along the way so that different aspects of each effect comes in and out to get as much variance as possible.” ~ Ola

The final parameter modifies the distortion on the voice, with various different crunching effects modifying and distorting the sound. Once again, we wanted to make players experiment, so as you move the slider along you’ll notice that certain aspects of the distortion lower in intensity while others pick up, varying independently to create unique sounds as you explore the possibilities!

“There is always a built-in effect on the voices, so they will never sound fully human. \[...] Even if you skip through the character creation and don’t mess with any of the dynamic settings, the character will still sound like a Skitarius.” ~ Ola

Bonus: What was your favorite part about working on the Skitarii?

“I do think that going deeper into what the character creation of the Skitarii looked like, and exploring the character under the suit, was especially interesting to me.” ~ Lucas

“I think it’s interesting to work on something this mechanical. It’s a different process! In some ways it’s more complex, because you have to make things mechanically work.” ~ Erik

“I think the class itself is really cool, there’s a lot of fun stuff you can do in regards to voice for the class. But this \[the voice modulation] is definitely, for me, the stand out fun feature. It’s been super fun tweaking these effects and having these moments where you go ‘ohh, this sounds really cool, this sounds really cool, let’s try that, oh that also sounds really cool!’.” ~ Ola

That’s all we have for today! We hope you enjoyed this early look at the character creation of the Skitarii class!

Stay tuned for the next feature highlight, which will focus on the Skitarii’s game design and talents, coming soon!!

Praise the Omnissiah!

The Skitarii Alpha Primus class will be available on the 23rd of June, but you can already wishlist it now. 

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355570/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class/https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355580/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class_Deluxe_Edition/

We’ll see you on the Mourningstar.

– The Darktide Team
item.content:encoded:
Rising above the shackles of the flesh, the Skitarii have been rebuilt with the divine augments of the Adeptus Mechanicus to best serve the Omnissiah, as an impossibly efficient tool of war.

In case you missed it, check out the reveal trailer here.

This is part of an ongoing series of feature highlights focused on the upcoming DLC class: The Skitarii.

In our previous highlight we went over the overall vision of the Skitarii’s visual design! This feature highlight focuses on unique options available to the class as far as character creation and customization goes, from unique physical options to voice modification! We’ve interviewed our Associate Art Director, 3D Technical Artist, and Sound Designer to get you all the details!

“My name is Lucas. I am the Associate Art Director on Darktide and I’ve been involved in overseeing the art delivered in the production of the Skitarii class, working closely together with the different disciplines in our production development team. \[...] As a huge Warhammer fan myself, getting the opportunity to explore something from the Adeptus Mechanicus faction was very exciting. ~Lucas

“My name is Erik Lindqvist, I’m a Senior Technical Artist at Fatshark. I’ve been at the company for about 10 years, mainly working on Vermintide and Vermintide 2, and now I’ve also been leading work on Skitarii on the Technical Art side.” ~ Erik

“I'm Ola, I'm the VO Designer at Darktide. I'm in charge of wrangling all the VO we record, processing it and getting it to play in the game.  Sometimes I show up to bother Matt and the actors with strange requests in a recording session. Other than that I like to get my fingers in some UI code on occasion (Personality Scourge and this upcoming Voxcaster Tuning for example).” ~Ola

Not Quite Human Anymore

“When we started talking and discussing about how we’d go about presenting this class, a lot of our ideas went naturally to the character creation of it.” ~ Lucas

While Arbites and Hive Scum fit quite comfortably using the base ‘human’ body as the core classes, the same isn’t true for the Skitarii. Due to their cybernetic nature, there were a lot of visual aspects we wanted to represent that warranted a brand new body type to go with it. 

But this also gave us a unique opportunity to show something rarely seen in other Warhammer: 40,000 media, which is how they look under the robes and the hood, something that the entire Art team was eager to work on.

“There wasn’t really an established visual reference for what a Skitarii looks like underneath the robes and armor, so that was a mystery which we needed to solve. The idea of them being flesh fused with machine raised a lot of questions. And because this was such an interesting hook for us to think about, it naturally evolved into a focus for the class!” ~ Lucas

Because this is rarely depicted, we found most of the relevant information (and inspiration!) in written narrative mediums – books, lore excerpts in codices, and so on. While we always collaborate closely with Games Workshop when bringing all aspects of Warhammer: 40,000 to life in Darktide, this was especially true when working on the Skitarii character creation. 

“I don’t think we left any ideas out. It was more the other way around, that we added ideas as we went!” ~ Lucas 

We worked extensively on concepts and design, shaping together the look of these seldom explored facets of the Adeptus Mechanicus. Things like how their bodies look after such extensive modification, the type of scarring left on their flesh after all the assignments and environmental hazards they’re often subjected to. How exactly it looks where machine meets flesh.

Customizing your Skitarius

As you might expect, many of the customization options available to the Skitarii are very different from our other classes, in part because of the fusion of man and machine, but also because they’ll be almost completely covered up once you’re done. We had to figure out how to let you add your personal touch in ways that would still be visible under those bulky robes, after all!

“The character creation flow became very different. We cannot use skin colors, we cannot use faces in the same way, to express the character. So we need to find something else.” ~ Erik

Like our other classes, you’ll get to pick from several background options to shape your character’s history, and you’ll get to tweak their height (although they are on average taller than the regular human classes!). Additionally, you’ll also be able to pick your Skitarius’ Forge World of origin, which plays a similar role as the Hive Scum’s choice of Gang, such as altering your starting cosmetics!

One of these Forge World options, Branx Minor, is linked to Atoma itself!

Eye color

Their natural eyes have long been replaced with bionic upgrades, giving you several color options to choose from! There will be a primary and a secondary lens color option, and of course you can pick the same option for both to have the lenses look the same. Not only that, but these colors will show up no matter what headgear cosmetic you end up using for your Skitarius!

“And that is a bit different depending on the headgear. Some only have two eyes, so you’re basically picking the right and the left eye. Some have smaller diodes and things, as well. Some only have one, so then there’s only the Primary that’s showing.” ~ Erik

Mechanical Limbs

The Skitarii’s arms, legs, hands and feet have been upgraded with cybernetic replacements, and during character creation these will also be customizable! You will be able to choose different models for the arms and legs, and you will also get to pick from different metallic textures as the Primary and Secondary metals, including a separate setting to handle the oxidation level.

These customizations will also remain and be visible no matter what cosmetics you’re using, giving you more freedom to craft a unique look. And of course, the arms and hands will also show up in first person!

Voice Modulation

The Skitarii class will have access to four distinct personalities, something only the Ogryn class can match! But, taking advantage of the Skitarii’s cybernetic nature, we added the ability to customize how the voice itself can sound!

The voice modulation system found in certain headgear cosmetics gave us a solid starting point for the feature, but we pushed the concept further, giving the player different ways to tune it to their heart’s content and find the perfect sound.

There are three different settings that you can gradually tweak to modify the base voice of the chosen personality. As you play around with these dynamic parameters, you’ll get to listen to the changes in real time through the example voice lines playing from your chosen personality, or even switch personalities to hear how specific setups sound on all the voices!

As you might expect, one of these parameters controls the pitch… among other things! While there is some correlation with increasing this slider to make the pitch higher, or decreasing it to make the pitch lower, it is not so straightforward. There are various effects being influenced by this parameter, purposely making the pitch shift unpredictably in certain ranges, and sometimes making the normal, unmodified voice shine through.

The second parameter is a delay effect, which can sound like multiple voices at once, or like the voice has an almost immediate echo. Like with the pitch parameter, modifying this parameter is not as straightforward as it might seem, affecting not just the intensity of the effect, but also other sound effects linked to it to create some unpredictable variations.

“You will hear stuff change as you move this around, but our goal is also to… You know, we want you to stumble into something that sounds cool, rather than necessarily be like ‘Oh this goes up and this goes there’, you know. \[...] We modulate it along the way so that different aspects of each effect comes in and out to get as much variance as possible.” ~ Ola

The final parameter modifies the distortion on the voice, with various different crunching effects modifying and distorting the sound. Once again, we wanted to make players experiment, so as you move the slider along you’ll notice that certain aspects of the distortion lower in intensity while others pick up, varying independently to create unique sounds as you explore the possibilities!

“There is always a built-in effect on the voices, so they will never sound fully human. \[...] Even if you skip through the character creation and don’t mess with any of the dynamic settings, the character will still sound like a Skitarius.” ~ Ola

Bonus: What was your favorite part about working on the Skitarii?

“I do think that going deeper into what the character creation of the Skitarii looked like, and exploring the character under the suit, was especially interesting to me.” ~ Lucas

“I think it’s interesting to work on something this mechanical. It’s a different process! In some ways it’s more complex, because you have to make things mechanically work.” ~ Erik

“I think the class itself is really cool, there’s a lot of fun stuff you can do in regards to voice for the class. But this \[the voice modulation] is definitely, for me, the stand out fun feature. It’s been super fun tweaking these effects and having these moments where you go ‘ohh, this sounds really cool, this sounds really cool, let’s try that, oh that also sounds really cool!’.” ~ Ola

That’s all we have for today! We hope you enjoyed this early look at the character creation of the Skitarii class!

Stay tuned for the next feature highlight, which will focus on the Skitarii’s game design and talents, coming soon!!

Praise the Omnissiah!

The Skitarii Alpha Primus class will be available on the 23rd of June, but you can already wishlist it now. 

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355570/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class/https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355580/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class_Deluxe_Edition/

We’ll see you on the Mourningstar.

– The Darktide Team

item.title: We Hear You – Bug Fix Update and Ongoing Optimisation Work
item.description:
First, thank you to everyone who has reported issues and shared feedback since the launch of Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus II.

We know we’ve been quiet since launch. That hasn’t been because we’re ignoring the issues being raised – it’s because the team has been focused on investigating, fixing and preparing updates as quickly as possible.

We’re painfully aware that many players are experiencing performance issues, including lower than expected frame rates and unusually high GPU utilisation. Improving performance is a critical priority for us, and work is continuing on a more substantial optimisation update.

Today’s update addresses a number of commonly reported bugs and includes a small FPS improvement for many players. However, we want to be clear that this update is not intended to resolve the main performance concerns raised by players. That work is ongoing and will require a further update.

We understand how frustrating these issues are, and we appreciate your patience while we work to address them. We’ll share more details on the optimisation update as soon as we’re able.

Update 1.0.0.25 Patch Notes

General

Implemented a change that results in a slight FPS improvement for many players.

Fixed an issue where the Shadow Quality setting could reset to High after relaunching the game.

Fixed a missing placeholder icon for some skills.

Fixed an issue preventing certain territory expansion missions in the Adeptus Mechanicus campaign from loading.

Audio

Fixed a number of SFX being incorrectly tied to VO volume.

Fixed some missing VO dialogue.

Fixed some instances of dialogue being wrongly assigned to different characters..

Gameplay

Fixed an issue where Videx’s Motive Force skill could grant an incorrect number of Energized stacks.

Fixed an issue in the Vindication skirmish where reaching the extraction area before killing all enemies could incorrectly result in a defeat.

Fixed an issue where triggering a Kastelan Robot’s Deadly Demise trait could skip allied unit turns and disrupt turn order.

Fixed an issue where recruiting a high number of units for a mission (19+) could prevent units from being deployed into a skirmish.

Fixed an issue where the Necron Deathmark Skill Improvement upgrade increased the cooldown of Hyper-dimensional Jump instead of reducing it.

Fixed an issue where the Expanded Sicarian Armour tooltip could obscure the upgrade button and prevent the upgrade from being selected.

Fixed an issue where Nanoscarab Cloud did not gain an additional use at Dominion 8.

Fixed an issue where The Hungry Void command protocol did not apply to the Voidblade.

Fixed an issue where multiple units could be deployed onto the same cell during the deployment phase.

Fixed an issue where Pteraxii Sterylizors had access to Purgation Protocol before unlocking it.

Fixed an issue where A Call for Flesh could sometimes fail to spawn a Flayed One.

Fixed an issue where Khepra’s Emergency Protocol skill could not be activated after being unlocked.

Achievements

Fixed an issue where the Tech-Acquisitor achievement could fail to unlock after reaching 10 Cognition Points with Scaevola.

Fixed an issue on Xbox where the achievement notification pop-up did not display correctly.
item.content:encoded:
First, thank you to everyone who has reported issues and shared feedback since the launch of Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus II.

We know we’ve been quiet since launch. That hasn’t been because we’re ignoring the issues being raised – it’s because the team has been focused on investigating, fixing and preparing updates as quickly as possible.

We’re painfully aware that many players are experiencing performance issues, including lower than expected frame rates and unusually high GPU utilisation. Improving performance is a critical priority for us, and work is continuing on a more substantial optimisation update.

Today’s update addresses a number of commonly reported bugs and includes a small FPS improvement for many players. However, we want to be clear that this update is not intended to resolve the main performance concerns raised by players. That work is ongoing and will require a further update.

We understand how frustrating these issues are, and we appreciate your patience while we work to address them. We’ll share more details on the optimisation update as soon as we’re able.

Update 1.0.0.25 Patch Notes

General

Implemented a change that results in a slight FPS improvement for many players.

Fixed an issue where the Shadow Quality setting could reset to High after relaunching the game.

Fixed a missing placeholder icon for some skills.

Fixed an issue preventing certain territory expansion missions in the Adeptus Mechanicus campaign from loading.

Audio

Fixed a number of SFX being incorrectly tied to VO volume.

Fixed some missing VO dialogue.

Fixed some instances of dialogue being wrongly assigned to different characters..

Gameplay

Fixed an issue where Videx’s Motive Force skill could grant an incorrect number of Energized stacks.

Fixed an issue in the Vindication skirmish where reaching the extraction area before killing all enemies could incorrectly result in a defeat.

Fixed an issue where triggering a Kastelan Robot’s Deadly Demise trait could skip allied unit turns and disrupt turn order.

Fixed an issue where recruiting a high number of units for a mission (19+) could prevent units from being deployed into a skirmish.

Fixed an issue where the Necron Deathmark Skill Improvement upgrade increased the cooldown of Hyper-dimensional Jump instead of reducing it.

Fixed an issue where the Expanded Sicarian Armour tooltip could obscure the upgrade button and prevent the upgrade from being selected.

Fixed an issue where Nanoscarab Cloud did not gain an additional use at Dominion 8.

Fixed an issue where The Hungry Void command protocol did not apply to the Voidblade.

Fixed an issue where multiple units could be deployed onto the same cell during the deployment phase.

Fixed an issue where Pteraxii Sterylizors had access to Purgation Protocol before unlocking it.

Fixed an issue where A Call for Flesh could sometimes fail to spawn a Flayed One.

Fixed an issue where Khepra’s Emergency Protocol skill could not be activated after being unlocked.

Achievements

Fixed an issue where the Tech-Acquisitor achievement could fail to unlock after reaching 10 Cognition Points with Scaevola.

Fixed an issue on Xbox where the achievement notification pop-up did not display correctly.

item.title: Hotfix 6.11.2 + 6.11.3 - 2nd of June
item.description:
Hotfix 6.11.2 - 2nd of June
Features & Tweaks

  • Huntsman’s Longbow no longer automatically zooms. 

  • Huntsman players can now press the Weapon Special button while aiming with a ranged weapon to get an extra zoom, regardless of whether they’re under the effects of their Career Skill or not.

Fixes

  • Fixed a crash when an Illusionist monster creates illusions and there is no one to see them.

  • Fixed a crash when sending a 500 character essay in a language other than English.

  • Fixed a crash that happened when hot-joining an ending adventure game and victory poses hadn't fully synchronized.

  • Fixed a bug where Sienna’s Mace L1/L2 burn VFX didn’t use the proper color when playing Necromancer.

  • Fixed a bug where Sienna’s Mace L1/L2 weren’t able to trigger the Soul Harvest talent.

  • Fixed a minor spelling mistake in the “Aim for the what?!” Okri’s Challenge.

  • Fixed a bug with the Axe & Shield H3 not being able to proc "On Charged Hit" Talents.

  • Sienna Mace H2/H3: Fix Drop-off curve hitting minimum damage on target 3 onward instead of target 4.

  • Removed a false Weapon Special action text on the Moonfire Bow’s weapon card.

  • Reverted unintended changes to Axe & Falchion H1/H2 and Sienna Dagger H2.

  • Reverted an unintended nerf to Hagbane Charged Shot direct hit stagger.

  • Fixed so that Monster Crit Damage on Crowbill H1/H2 was aligned to the lower of the two values from pre-patch instead of the higher.

Hotfix 6.11.3 - 2nd of June
Fixes

  • Fixed a crash when trying to join a friend in the main menu instead of the keep.
item.content:encoded:
Hotfix 6.11.2 - 2nd of June
Features & Tweaks

  • Huntsman’s Longbow no longer automatically zooms. 

  • Huntsman players can now press the Weapon Special button while aiming with a ranged weapon to get an extra zoom, regardless of whether they’re under the effects of their Career Skill or not.

Fixes

  • Fixed a crash when an Illusionist monster creates illusions and there is no one to see them.

  • Fixed a crash when sending a 500 character essay in a language other than English.

  • Fixed a crash that happened when hot-joining an ending adventure game and victory poses hadn't fully synchronized.

  • Fixed a bug where Sienna’s Mace L1/L2 burn VFX didn’t use the proper color when playing Necromancer.

  • Fixed a bug where Sienna’s Mace L1/L2 weren’t able to trigger the Soul Harvest talent.

  • Fixed a minor spelling mistake in the “Aim for the what?!” Okri’s Challenge.

  • Fixed a bug with the Axe & Shield H3 not being able to proc "On Charged Hit" Talents.

  • Sienna Mace H2/H3: Fix Drop-off curve hitting minimum damage on target 3 onward instead of target 4.

  • Removed a false Weapon Special action text on the Moonfire Bow’s weapon card.

  • Reverted unintended changes to Axe & Falchion H1/H2 and Sienna Dagger H2.

  • Reverted an unintended nerf to Hagbane Charged Shot direct hit stagger.

  • Fixed so that Monster Crit Damage on Crowbill H1/H2 was aligned to the lower of the two values from pre-patch instead of the higher.

Hotfix 6.11.3 - 2nd of June
Fixes

  • Fixed a crash when trying to join a friend in the main menu instead of the keep.

item.title: Hotfix 8.0.2
item.description:
Hey folks,

We have another Hotfix for you today, including some cost balancing to Tiger Warriors and some crash fixes, as well as some additional bug squashing and adjustments.

As always, if you run into any new issues after updating please report them over in our bug reporting forum.

See you on the battlefield!

— Steve // Senior Community Manager

 

Campaign

Cathay/Tiger Warriors:

  • Reduced campaign costs for some Tiger Warrior units:

    • Tiger Warrior Iron Claws:

      • Recruitment = 1700 > 1600 per unit

    • Tiger Warrior Stalkers:

      • Recruitment = 1550 > 1400 per unit

      • Upkeep = 300 > 275 per unit

    • Tiger Warrior Dual Axes:

      • Recruitment = 1200 > 1000 per unit

      • Upkeep = 250 > 225 per unit

  • Changed the Desert Tigress Insignia for Jade Crossbows benefit to Armour-piercing missile weapon damage; Increased effect benefit from 3 to 4

  • Increased Desert Tigress Insignia for Jade Infantry Armour-piercing effect from 3 to 4

  • Made completing Zhao Ming’s goals more rewarding by increasing the drop chance for higher tier units when accomplishing them

  • Fixed a crash on end of turn while playing as Bhashiva

  • Fixed a crash that would occur when opening the Armies of Shang-Yang panel

  • Fixed an issue so that the Clawspeaker Hero provided to Caravan Lords by the Deeply Devout trait now correctly start with spells and abilities unlocked

  • Fixed an issue that would occur when capturing settlements from rebels with Bhashiva

  • Campaign movement range bonus has now replaced "Strider" from the "Fleet of Foot" traits since "Wayfarer" under the same trait already provides "Strider"

  • Tiger Warriors now receive proper recruitment cost reduction from Prophecy of the White Tiger buildings

  • Fixed a bug so that benefits targeting Melee infantry provided by Zhao Ming's skills and faction effects now include Tiger Warriors

  • Fixed an issue so that Bhashiva's direct allies are no longer targets of Zhao Ming's Goals

  • Fixed caravans being counted towards the army count in the faction summary screen

  • Fixed the banner upgrades menu closing instantly after opening if you have an agent selected before opening it

  • Improved consistency when generating Zhao's Goals

  • Fixed error in mission execution for Bhashiva around the Chaos Invasions

  • Interacting with Zhao Ming's end turn goals now opens the missions tab instead of the default units tab in AoSY

  • Fixed an issue where the text pointer would not fire as intended on the Armies of Shang-Yang panel

  • Fixed a rare post-battle crash after Miao Ying’s Realm of Chaos Battle

  • Fixed an issue with an animation where Auto resolving battles with Lord Magistrate while he is on a Sky Lantern mount

  • Fixed an issue where Armies of Shang-Yang missions were not displaying unit cards for the relevant unit requirements

  • Fixed an issue where the Insignia assign menu loses functionality if it's opened whilst the army is moving

Other:

  • Fixed an issue where some Norscan homeland settlements were incorrectly set up, causing Warriors of Chaos factions to be unable to vassalize their respective Norscan factions when conquering the settlements

  • Fixed an issue so that When playing as Skarbrand in the Realms of Chaos campaign, the mission "Chaos Invasion: Despoilers" should now spawn a fully functional Cultist of Khorne. Note that this will not affect save games where the mission has been already started or completed

  • Fixed an issue so that Epidemius' Tally of Pestilence should now properly count plagues that spread to settlements

Battle

  • Resolved a problem where the AI was constantly prioritizing single entity targets for AOE spells

  • Fix a softlock that would occur when opening the Unit & Spell browser after a Mirror of Madness battle

  • Fixed an issue so that the Sky Lantern Gunner crew's effective range is now aligned with the sky lantern's

  • Fixed an issues with Bhashiva's teleport attacks not playing their sound effects in quest battles

⚠ IMPORTANT

If you experience any issues after downloading the update, please take a moment to Verify the integrity of the game files first to ensure there were no issues when downloading. A step-by-step guide can be found in this article.

If the issues persist, please visit our support site for additional troubleshooting steps and assistance.

🧱 MOD WARNING

Please note that user-generated mods may have compatibility issues with new game updates. If you are encountering performance or stability issues following a release, is recommended that you disable any mods you're running. If you continue to experience issues, please visit our support site for additional troubleshooting steps and assistance.

For the latest news and announcements, drop by our official social channels.

SteamXFacebookInstagramDiscordBluesky
item.content:encoded:
Hey folks,

We have another Hotfix for you today, including some cost balancing to Tiger Warriors and some crash fixes, as well as some additional bug squashing and adjustments.

As always, if you run into any new issues after updating please report them over in our bug reporting forum.

See you on the battlefield!

— Steve // Senior Community Manager

 

Campaign

Cathay/Tiger Warriors:

  • Reduced campaign costs for some Tiger Warrior units:

    • Tiger Warrior Iron Claws:

      • Recruitment = 1700 > 1600 per unit

    • Tiger Warrior Stalkers:

      • Recruitment = 1550 > 1400 per unit

      • Upkeep = 300 > 275 per unit

    • Tiger Warrior Dual Axes:

      • Recruitment = 1200 > 1000 per unit

      • Upkeep = 250 > 225 per unit

  • Changed the Desert Tigress Insignia for Jade Crossbows benefit to Armour-piercing missile weapon damage; Increased effect benefit from 3 to 4

  • Increased Desert Tigress Insignia for Jade Infantry Armour-piercing effect from 3 to 4

  • Made completing Zhao Ming’s goals more rewarding by increasing the drop chance for higher tier units when accomplishing them

  • Fixed a crash on end of turn while playing as Bhashiva

  • Fixed a crash that would occur when opening the Armies of Shang-Yang panel

  • Fixed an issue so that the Clawspeaker Hero provided to Caravan Lords by the Deeply Devout trait now correctly start with spells and abilities unlocked

  • Fixed an issue that would occur when capturing settlements from rebels with Bhashiva

  • Campaign movement range bonus has now replaced "Strider" from the "Fleet of Foot" traits since "Wayfarer" under the same trait already provides "Strider"

  • Tiger Warriors now receive proper recruitment cost reduction from Prophecy of the White Tiger buildings

  • Fixed a bug so that benefits targeting Melee infantry provided by Zhao Ming's skills and faction effects now include Tiger Warriors

  • Fixed an issue so that Bhashiva's direct allies are no longer targets of Zhao Ming's Goals

  • Fixed caravans being counted towards the army count in the faction summary screen

  • Fixed the banner upgrades menu closing instantly after opening if you have an agent selected before opening it

  • Improved consistency when generating Zhao's Goals

  • Fixed error in mission execution for Bhashiva around the Chaos Invasions

  • Interacting with Zhao Ming's end turn goals now opens the missions tab instead of the default units tab in AoSY

  • Fixed an issue where the text pointer would not fire as intended on the Armies of Shang-Yang panel

  • Fixed a rare post-battle crash after Miao Ying’s Realm of Chaos Battle

  • Fixed an issue with an animation where Auto resolving battles with Lord Magistrate while he is on a Sky Lantern mount

  • Fixed an issue where Armies of Shang-Yang missions were not displaying unit cards for the relevant unit requirements

  • Fixed an issue where the Insignia assign menu loses functionality if it's opened whilst the army is moving

Other:

  • Fixed an issue where some Norscan homeland settlements were incorrectly set up, causing Warriors of Chaos factions to be unable to vassalize their respective Norscan factions when conquering the settlements

  • Fixed an issue so that When playing as Skarbrand in the Realms of Chaos campaign, the mission "Chaos Invasion: Despoilers" should now spawn a fully functional Cultist of Khorne. Note that this will not affect save games where the mission has been already started or completed

  • Fixed an issue so that Epidemius' Tally of Pestilence should now properly count plagues that spread to settlements

Battle

  • Resolved a problem where the AI was constantly prioritizing single entity targets for AOE spells

  • Fix a softlock that would occur when opening the Unit & Spell browser after a Mirror of Madness battle

  • Fixed an issue so that the Sky Lantern Gunner crew's effective range is now aligned with the sky lantern's

  • Fixed an issues with Bhashiva's teleport attacks not playing their sound effects in quest battles

⚠ IMPORTANT

If you experience any issues after downloading the update, please take a moment to Verify the integrity of the game files first to ensure there were no issues when downloading. A step-by-step guide can be found in this article.

If the issues persist, please visit our support site for additional troubleshooting steps and assistance.

🧱 MOD WARNING

Please note that user-generated mods may have compatibility issues with new game updates. If you are encountering performance or stability issues following a release, is recommended that you disable any mods you're running. If you continue to experience issues, please visit our support site for additional troubleshooting steps and assistance.

For the latest news and announcements, drop by our official social channels.

SteamXFacebookInstagramDiscordBluesky

item.title: Feature Highlight - Skitarii Visual Design
item.description:
Warband!

Last week we revealed the new Skitarii Alpha Primus class to the world. We are so excited for these specialist warriors of the Machine-God to join the fight on Atoma, bringing their wargear and combat expertise to the fight against the heretics!

Today we are joined by Matte and Lucas, our Art Director and Associate Art Director, to chat a bit about how we visualised this new class in Darktide.

Take it away guys! 

“As a huge Warhammer fan myself, getting the opportunity to explore something from the Adeptus Mechanicus faction was very exciting”

  • Lucas

We already touched upon the fact that the entire Darktide team has been waiting for a chance to add an Adeptus Mechanicus themed class to the game for a long time, and for many of us this really is a dream come true.

The ritualistic elements of this faction, combined with their technology is something we find really interesting, and getting to pour all of our combined knowledge and ideas into this has been such a fun journey. It’s been great to collaborate together with Games Workshop to make sure we get the look and feel of these warriors just right. 

We realised early that if we are doing an Adeptus Mechanicus class, it would be represented by the Skitarii, as they make the most sense, both for the power level of the Inquisitorial Warband, but also from a good gameplay experience perspective. 

“I think this is the closest we have been to the core of Warhammer 40.000 for a class in the game” 

  • Matte

Since the Skitarii are so iconic for Warhammer 40.000, we knew that this would be an immense task to represent in the game. Not only did we want to honor the lore, but we also wanted to make sure that the class ticked all the boxes, being represented correctly and fun to play!

Skitarii headgear concept

Skitarii backpack concept

One of the most interesting (and important) parts of the Skitarii is the blend between human and machine. The Skitarii are at their core humans, but they have been augmented by bionics to better suit the battlefield conditions they are faced with. Their limbs have been upgraded to mechanical ones, and their faces are covered in different optical enhancements and other gruesome details, all to make them better warriors. We thought a lot on how to represent this, and we hope that when you get to see the coming reveals, you can see how much care and consideration went into this.

Bionic arm concept 

The bionics themselves have been very fun to work on. Since we have always hoped to bring this class to the game, we have consciously not added too much of these to the cosmetics of the other classes. We felt that if we ever get the chance to visualise the Adeptus Mechanicus as a player class, that was going to be the time to go all out on this.

And that time is finally here!

“This has been a project long vision for the team, and I was excited for us to finalise this class”

  • Matte

Another really cool thing to work on was their iconic weaponry and adding some of them to the game. It is always a challenge to take something from the tabletop game and bring it to life. The weapons are front and center in Darktide, so it’s something we take great pride in trying to figure out how to represent in the best way possible.

Phosphor Blast Pistol firing & reload concept 

Together with Games Workshop, we always try to make the weapons believable, especially the ranged ones. A lot of work goes into trying to figure out how parts move, how the reload mechanism works and how the weapon acts as you pull the trigger. Not to mention the sound and VFX of the shots.

The weapons of the Adeptus Mechanicus are works of art, and we spent a lot of time making sure that this is also shown. The blend between high-tech, ornate wood work and fine metals make them really stand out. We thought along the lines of making them feel both archaic and advanced at the same time. 

Galvanic Rifle Concept 

One good example of this is the Galvanic Rifle. We took the existing concept art, the look of the miniatures themselves and then mixed this with references of how historical weapons work and sprinkled the advanced technology of the Adeptus Mechanicus on top.  This all blends together to make the weapon you will see in the game. It’s a long process to get everything right, but a very rewarding one when it’s done.

“There is also the gameplay aspect of it, we want to make sure the weapons look and act in a way that makes sense for the player while using them”

  • Matte

Another huge task was to visualise the room where you will create your player character. It’s the inner sanctum of the old Adeptus Mechanicus facility that Hadron Omega-7-7 has gotten access to, and it is here standard Skitarii are upgraded into the Alpha Primus variant. 

Skitarii Alpha Primus character creator ideas 

There is so much detail that has gone into this. First and foremost we wanted the mood of it to be just right. In the center there is this giant machine, the main component of the upgrade process, but the room is also filled with censer smoke and candles. We really tried to nail the feel of this being both a machine and ritual chamber. 

Skitarii human parts concept

It is here you will see the full display of the gruesome nature of the Skitarii. Suspended in the middle will be a disfigured torso and head that is the core of your character, and you get to pick the mechanical limbs and other details for it. We wanted it to feel like you are creating your very own Skitarii. 

“The hood, the face and the bionic limbs are so iconic” 

  • Lucas

Much of this will then be concealed by the hood and robes of the Adeptus Mechanicus when you are done. And since you also get to pick what Forge World your Skitarii initially came from, these come in some different colors to tie it all together.

Forge World color variations (Mars, Ryza & Branx Minor)

That’s as much as we will go into today, all in all this has been a very challenging, but rewarding, process for us, and we have so much to share with you in the upcoming weeks! 

If you want to know more about the character customization, make sure to tune in next week as we will really deep dive into this. But for now, this is us signing off.


The Skitarii Alpha Primus class will be available on the 23rd of June, but you can already wishlist it now.

 

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355570/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class/https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355580/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class_Deluxe_Edition/Thanks to Matte and Lucas for sharing their thoughts and experience on the visual design of the new class, and thanks to all of you for reading! 

Praise the Omnissiah!

See you at the Mourningstar!

- The Darktide team
item.content:encoded:
Warband!

Last week we revealed the new Skitarii Alpha Primus class to the world. We are so excited for these specialist warriors of the Machine-God to join the fight on Atoma, bringing their wargear and combat expertise to the fight against the heretics!

Today we are joined by Matte and Lucas, our Art Director and Associate Art Director, to chat a bit about how we visualised this new class in Darktide.

Take it away guys! 

“As a huge Warhammer fan myself, getting the opportunity to explore something from the Adeptus Mechanicus faction was very exciting”

  • Lucas

We already touched upon the fact that the entire Darktide team has been waiting for a chance to add an Adeptus Mechanicus themed class to the game for a long time, and for many of us this really is a dream come true.

The ritualistic elements of this faction, combined with their technology is something we find really interesting, and getting to pour all of our combined knowledge and ideas into this has been such a fun journey. It’s been great to collaborate together with Games Workshop to make sure we get the look and feel of these warriors just right. 

We realised early that if we are doing an Adeptus Mechanicus class, it would be represented by the Skitarii, as they make the most sense, both for the power level of the Inquisitorial Warband, but also from a good gameplay experience perspective. 

“I think this is the closest we have been to the core of Warhammer 40.000 for a class in the game” 

  • Matte

Since the Skitarii are so iconic for Warhammer 40.000, we knew that this would be an immense task to represent in the game. Not only did we want to honor the lore, but we also wanted to make sure that the class ticked all the boxes, being represented correctly and fun to play!

Skitarii headgear concept

Skitarii backpack concept

One of the most interesting (and important) parts of the Skitarii is the blend between human and machine. The Skitarii are at their core humans, but they have been augmented by bionics to better suit the battlefield conditions they are faced with. Their limbs have been upgraded to mechanical ones, and their faces are covered in different optical enhancements and other gruesome details, all to make them better warriors. We thought a lot on how to represent this, and we hope that when you get to see the coming reveals, you can see how much care and consideration went into this.

Bionic arm concept 

The bionics themselves have been very fun to work on. Since we have always hoped to bring this class to the game, we have consciously not added too much of these to the cosmetics of the other classes. We felt that if we ever get the chance to visualise the Adeptus Mechanicus as a player class, that was going to be the time to go all out on this.

And that time is finally here!

“This has been a project long vision for the team, and I was excited for us to finalise this class”

  • Matte

Another really cool thing to work on was their iconic weaponry and adding some of them to the game. It is always a challenge to take something from the tabletop game and bring it to life. The weapons are front and center in Darktide, so it’s something we take great pride in trying to figure out how to represent in the best way possible.

Phosphor Blast Pistol firing & reload concept 

Together with Games Workshop, we always try to make the weapons believable, especially the ranged ones. A lot of work goes into trying to figure out how parts move, how the reload mechanism works and how the weapon acts as you pull the trigger. Not to mention the sound and VFX of the shots.

The weapons of the Adeptus Mechanicus are works of art, and we spent a lot of time making sure that this is also shown. The blend between high-tech, ornate wood work and fine metals make them really stand out. We thought along the lines of making them feel both archaic and advanced at the same time. 

Galvanic Rifle Concept 

One good example of this is the Galvanic Rifle. We took the existing concept art, the look of the miniatures themselves and then mixed this with references of how historical weapons work and sprinkled the advanced technology of the Adeptus Mechanicus on top.  This all blends together to make the weapon you will see in the game. It’s a long process to get everything right, but a very rewarding one when it’s done.

“There is also the gameplay aspect of it, we want to make sure the weapons look and act in a way that makes sense for the player while using them”

  • Matte

Another huge task was to visualise the room where you will create your player character. It’s the inner sanctum of the old Adeptus Mechanicus facility that Hadron Omega-7-7 has gotten access to, and it is here standard Skitarii are upgraded into the Alpha Primus variant. 

Skitarii Alpha Primus character creator ideas 

There is so much detail that has gone into this. First and foremost we wanted the mood of it to be just right. In the center there is this giant machine, the main component of the upgrade process, but the room is also filled with censer smoke and candles. We really tried to nail the feel of this being both a machine and ritual chamber. 

Skitarii human parts concept

It is here you will see the full display of the gruesome nature of the Skitarii. Suspended in the middle will be a disfigured torso and head that is the core of your character, and you get to pick the mechanical limbs and other details for it. We wanted it to feel like you are creating your very own Skitarii. 

“The hood, the face and the bionic limbs are so iconic” 

  • Lucas

Much of this will then be concealed by the hood and robes of the Adeptus Mechanicus when you are done. And since you also get to pick what Forge World your Skitarii initially came from, these come in some different colors to tie it all together.

Forge World color variations (Mars, Ryza & Branx Minor)

That’s as much as we will go into today, all in all this has been a very challenging, but rewarding, process for us, and we have so much to share with you in the upcoming weeks! 

If you want to know more about the character customization, make sure to tune in next week as we will really deep dive into this. But for now, this is us signing off.


The Skitarii Alpha Primus class will be available on the 23rd of June, but you can already wishlist it now.

 

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355570/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class/https://store.steampowered.com/app/4355580/Warhammer_40000_Darktide__Skitarii_Class_Deluxe_Edition/Thanks to Matte and Lucas for sharing their thoughts and experience on the visual design of the new class, and thanks to all of you for reading! 

Praise the Omnissiah!

See you at the Mourningstar!

- The Darktide team
